posts.title: The 21-Day Habit Myth: What Science Says
posts.excerpt: How long it actually takes to form a new habit.
posts.content:
The 21-day rule is a myth. Here's what research shows about habit formation.

The Real Number

Average is 66 days, ranging from 18 to 254.

Consistency Matters

Missing one day doesn't ruin progress.
posts.author: Brian Mitchell

posts.title: Habit Stacking: How to Build Routines That Stick
posts.excerpt: Leverage existing habits to build new ones.
posts.content:
Habit stacking pairs new behaviors with established routines. Here's how.

Identify a Stable Habit

Brushing teeth, morning coffee, etc.

Attach Your New Habit

After [existing habit], I will [new habit].
posts.author: Brian Mitchell

posts.title: Fixed vs Growth Mindset: What Carol Dweck Got Right
posts.excerpt: Understanding the difference between fixed and growth mindsets.
posts.content:
Your mindset shapes your potential. Here's how to develop a growth mindset.

Embrace Challenges

See difficulty as opportunity.

Learn from Criticism

Feedback is information, not judgment.
posts.author: Amanda White

posts.title: The Stoic Art of Living
posts.excerpt: Ancient wisdom from Marcus Aurelius, Seneca, and Epictetus for modern life.
posts.content:
Stoicism offers practical tools for resilience and peace. Here are key principles.

Focus on What You Control

Your thoughts and actions, not external events.

Practice Negative Visualization

Imagine loss to appreciate what you have.
posts.author: Amanda White

posts.title: Overcoming Imposter Syndrome
posts.excerpt: Practical strategies for dealing with self-doubt and inadequacy.
posts.content:

Recognize the feeling when it appears.

Collect Evidence

Document your accomplishments and positive feedback.

posts.title: The Anti-Networking Guide: Building Real Relationships
posts.excerpt: How to connect with people without feeling fake or transactional.
posts.content:

Help others without expecting immediate return.

Be Genuinely Curious

Ask questions and listen.

posts.title: How to Ask for a Raise
posts.excerpt: A step-by-step guide to negotiating your salary.
posts.content:

Know what you're worth.

Document Your Wins

Track your contributions and impact.

posts.title: Switching Careers: A Practical Guide
posts.excerpt: How to transition to a new field without starting from scratch.

Find positions that combine old and new.

posts.excerpt: What Lincoln, Churchill, and other leaders can teach us.
posts.content:

Communicate Clearly

Simple, consistent messaging wins.

posts.title: Emotional Intelligence for Leaders
posts.excerpt: Why EQ matters more than IQ for effective leadership.
posts.content:

Empathy

Understand others' perspectives.

posts.title: Building High-Performing Teams
posts.excerpt: The five characteristics of effective teams.

Clear Goals

posts.title: Crucial Conversations: How to Handle Tough Talks
posts.excerpt: A framework for navigating high-stakes discussions.

Clarify your true purpose.

Stay Curious

Seek to understand before being understood.

posts.title: Active Listening: The Most Underrated Skill
posts.excerpt: How to listen in a way that makes people feel heard.
posts.content:

Ask Open Questions

Encourage elaboration.

posts.title: Giving and Receiving Feedback
posts.excerpt: How to make feedback constructive, not destructive.
posts.content:

posts.title: The 30-Day Minimalism Challenge
posts.excerpt: One month to declutter your home and mind.

Week 2: Books and Media

Declutter your bookshelf and digital files.

posts.title: Digital Minimalism: Reclaiming Your Attention

Attachment causes suffering. Letting go brings peace.

Stoicism

Focus on what you control, not what you own.
